Video.summary: Time-saving quick tips to feed your crafting craving.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Learn to sew, step-by-step with National Sewing Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.grandparentTitle: National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Welcome to Artists's Academy where we are dedicated to making you a better artist. We believe everyone has an artist within, waiting to be unleashed. Our mission is to be your ultimate resource for visual art education using tutorials covering everything from fundamental techniques to advanced concepts in painting and drawing.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Learn to knit, step-by-step with The Knitting Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: First, Dave Munkittrick demonstrates a handy tip for creating easy-to-use sanding pads. Then, George Vondriska and Matt Newborg discuss how to achieve a high-gloss finish on your woodworking projects. Learn to prevent over-sanding and troubleshoot imperfections in your wood surface with their expert tips.
Video.grandparentTitle: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Achieving a High-Gloss Finish

Video.summary: Great cuts make for more accurate quilts, and Angela is here to share some tips and tricks for quick and precise fabric cutting. Plus, she shares the best ruler sizes to have on hand.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Creative Crochet Corner is an online video streaming service all about learning how to crochet! Whether you're a new to crochet just learning the basics or an experienced crocheter looking for a challenge, Creative Crochet Corner is here to help. We offer a vast library of video tutorials taught by talented crochet experts who guide you through each technique step-by-step, sharing their best tips and tricks.
Video.grandparentTitle: Creative Crochet Corner presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: With a few supplies and cold connection techniques, designer Abbi Berta quickly creates mixed media earrings. She also makes a textured cuff with a torch-fired patina to introduce metalwork. Viewer Leslie Rocha tells how her first jewelry class and love of music blossomed into a passion for making jewelry from guitar wire and picks.
Video.title: Getting Started in Jewelry Making

Video.summary: Not all artists use brushes, and paint is not their only medium of choice. On today's episode, we begin with a lesson inspired by Camille Pissarro as we paint using a palette knife instead of a brush. Then meet Dina Wakley for a graffiti inspired, modern street art design. We finish up with Cheryl Boglioli and a 3D sculpture using an interesting medium to create a female form reminiscent of modernism.
Video.title: Mediums

Video.summary: Spike Carlsen joins George Vondriska in the workshop to show you how to make your own timeless, craftsman style table, inspired by Spike's uncle Bob. Spike shares a little bit about the inspiration behind his side table before demonstrating the cuts and assembly needed to bring this simple yet elegant project to life.
Video.grandparentTitle: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Uncle Bob's Craftsman Side Table

Video.summary: Create a set of stylish stools with George Vondriska's step-by-step guidance as he breaks down the construction of these attractive pieces. Learn to use your table saw to make the cuts you'll need, then see how to assemble your stool and and add decorative details for professional looking results.
Video.grandparentTitle: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Stylish Stool

Video.summary: Every occasion no matter how big or small deserves a card – and today we've got designs for everyday occasions. Today's soup includes lacy pop up cards with a graphic design, some tasty treat cards featuring a lemonade stand with Sara Naumann, and translating a layout into a card with Nancy Nally. Lots of cards and ingredients in one Scrapbook Soup.
Video.title: Cards for Everyday Occasions

Video.summary: Join George Vondriska to learn how to make your own end-grain cutting board that's sure to impress. Learn which types of wood work best for cutting boards and which materials to avoid when crafting anything that comes in contact with food. Find out how to properly glue, sand, and cut your pieces for a flat, even surface and achieve a finished product that is truly a cut above the rest.
Video.grandparentTitle: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: End-Grain Cutting Board

Video.summary: Expand basic piecing techniques. Author and designer Anna Maria Horner shares ideas for combining piecing with applique techniques. Free-motion quilting artist Christina Cameli stitches Lazy Eights. Textile artist Sarah Sharp adds interest and color to solid blocks with pieced strips.
Video.title: More Than Piecing

Video.summary: Ready to find your center? Tonight, Angela becomes one with the piecing as she makes a stress-relieving half-square triangle quilt.
Video.title: Stress Relieving Half-Square Triangle Quilt

Video.summary: Angela whips up a stash-busting table topper using the quilt-as-you-go method Gudrun Erla teaches in her online class "Fast & Furious: Quilt-As-You-Go With Pre-Cuts."
Video.title: Stash Busting Sunburst Table Topper

Video.summary: Forget milk and cookies, in Angela's house, it's all about the wine and popcorn. Spend the night before Christmas piecing triangles and playing with negative space while you wait up for Santa with Angela.
Video.title: Santorini

Video.summary: The Walters household is eating healthy this week, but that doesn't mean Angela's quilts have to be. Today she is whipping up the Milky Way Quilt by Monique Dillard featuring Lily & Loom fabric.
Video.title: Milky Way

Video.summary: All quilters can get stuck in a creative rut, even Angela. This week, she turns to strip piecing and her fun, easy-to-piece chevron pattern to get her quilting mojo back. Plus, she'll show you how to use negative space to bring your quilt to life.
Video.title: Strip-Pieced Contemporary Chevron Quilt

Video.summary: Holy scrap. Quilting can get a little messy sometimes, especially when you're busting your scrap stash. But nothing's gonna stop Angela from making room for new fabric! Feel the same? Your stitching sidekick awaits. (She's even sharing the free quilt pattern!)
Video.title: Shattered Frames

Video.summary: Angela's been challenged by The Crafty Gemini to make a stash-busting tote bag. So she's stepping outside her comfort zone and up to the #MidnightStashChallenge!
Video.title: Quilted Tote Bag Challenge

Video.summary: Don't you love that feeling when you use up every bit of fabric for a quilt? Tune in as Angela blazes the path to a stash-neutral quilt that leaves no scrap behind.
Video.title: Pathfinder Quilt

Video.summary: Believe it or not, this quilt is only made of straight seams! Watch it "unfold" as Angela conquers the Strip City pattern with Boundless blues.
Video.title: Strip City

Video.summary: Learn to quilt step by step with National Quilting Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.title: National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Image.alt: National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Video.summary: Sometimes you need a little extra warmth; that’s where fashionable sweaters and sweatshirts can be that add on topper. The show opens with blogger, Emily Thompson and her amazing sweater transformation using a serger. Then Cheryl Sleboda transforms any solid color sweatshirt into your favorite animal - from baby to adult sizes. The show closes with Angela Wolf and the next Sew Along lesson of the trench coat - adding patch pockets.
Video.title: Warm Up

Video.summary: Sewing experts offer easy ways to create neat sewing projects right at home. Viewers of all skill levels learn tips and techniques for creating professional-looking garments, personalized apparel and home accents. Weekly episodes feature various themes, and feature two main projects and one shorter technique segment. Some projects include monogramming napkins and towels; baby poncho, hooded towel and baby-friendly diapers; embellishing yoga pants with a clothing tattoo; and sewing on knits.
Video.title: It's Sew Easy

Video.summary: Add new materials to your training routine. Teacher and designer Steven James warms up by forming wire into words and coating them with enamel. In the work out, he adds water and salt to traditional enameling techniques for fabulous results. Mixed media artist Susan Lenart ices things down by adding foil and glitter to resin to create druzy designs.
Video.title: Enamel Training

Video.summary: When you talk about art, we often imagine a painstaking process that takes a lot of time, but today's artists are here to show speedy techniques that still create beautiful works of art! Wyatt White starts us out with 4 projects. They are super-fast and introduce a new technique. Then Eva Sherman has tips for soldering jump rings, make a lot to start and then get creative. Tammy Honaman finishes up with ways to bring mixed media into your jewelry with resin. The process is super-fast minus the drying time.
Video.title: Time for Art

Video.summary: Today's menu features useful 3D scrapbook projects. We make pencils with an artful touch, a scrapbook grid layout to celebrate daily life with Ali Edwards, and a scrapbook inspired tote using kaleidoscopes with Joe Rotella. Stir them all together for one fabulous Scrapbook Soup.
Video.title: 3D Projects

Video.summary: Once it's built, it's time for the grand opening! Dress up with wood and clay jewelry holder from Candie Cooper. Jen Cushman stamps an on-trend metal bracelet. Rae Missigman makes a cute purse from recycled products. Mary Hettsmansperger has safety tips for working in your studio.
Video.title: Grand Opening - Proper Attire

Video.summary: Did you know there is more than one way to do a yarn over? Yup, there are actually four! This week Tracey and Jodi demonstrate all four techniques and talk about when to use each.
Video.title: Four Ways to Yarn Over in Pattern & Lace Knitting Demystified

Image.alt: Four Ways to Yarn Over in Pattern & Lace Knitting Demystified
Four Ways to Yarn Over in Pattern & Lace Knitting Demystified
Four Ways to Yarn Over in Pattern & Lace Knitting Demystified

Video.summary: Did you know there is more than one way to do a yarn over? Yup, there are actually four! This week Tracey and Jodi demonstrate all four techniques and talk about when to use each.
Video.title: Four Ways to Yarn Over in Pattern & Lace Knitting Demystified

Image.alt: Four Ways to Yarn Over in Pattern & Lace Knitting Demystified
Four Ways to Yarn Over in Pattern & Lace Knitting Demystified
Four Ways to Yarn Over in Pattern & Lace Knitting Demystified

Video.summary: Time-saving quick tips to feed your crafting craving.
Video.title: Craft Hacks

Video.summary: Look beyond the sky. Jenny Barnett-Rohrs makes a glittery star catcher featuring the constellations. Candie Cooper creates a 3-D board illustrating moon phases. Artist Franz Spohn creates watercolor paintings featuring clouds. Jenny makes a suncatcher mandala and Candie Cooper captures a sparkly space galaxy in a bottle.
Video.title: Stars

Video.summary: Learn to bake and cook with CraftsyTV.
Video.title: Baking & Cooking

Video.summary: Welcome to CraftsyTV's Cook & Create, where delectable delights meet crafty creators.
Video.title: Cook & Create
